REFERENCE: The reference appears to be real and authentic, with one notable error. The large author list (53 authors) is consistent with a high-impact Cell paper involving multi-institutional collaboration. The title is scientifically coherent and appropriate for a colorectal cancer/journal paper. The DOI (10.1016/j.cell.2021.11.031) format is valid and points to a Cell-published work. The PMID (34910928) and PMCID (PMC8941949) follow correct formatting. However, the journal name is given as 'Celp' — this is an obvious typo and should be 'Cell'. The volume number 184 is plausible for Cell in 2022. The authors include well-known researchers (e.g., Coffey RJ, Regev A, Rozenblatt-Rosen O, Lau KS) associated with institutions like Vanderbilt University and the Broad Institute, consistent with the subject matter. Conclusion: the paper is real; the only error is the misspelled journal name.
authors: The 53-author list is long but plausible for a large-scale collaborative study published in a top-tier journal like Cell. Many of the authors (e.g., Coffey RJ, Franklin JL, Lau KS, Shrubsole MJ) are associated with Vanderbilt University and colorectal cancer research; others (e.g., Regev A, Rozenblatt-Rosen O, Hacohen N, Giannakis M) are affiliated with MIT/Broad Institute and are well-known in genomics and cancer immunology. No obviously fictitious names are detected.
title: The title 'Differential pre-malignant programs and microenvironment chart distinct paths to malignancy in human colorectal polyps' is scientific, specific, and consistent with known work in colorectal cancer epigenetics and microenvironment research. It matches the expected author collaborations and the journal Cell.
journal_name: The journal name 'Celp' is an obvious typo for 'Cell'. All other bibliographic elements (DOI prefix, PMID, author affiliations, subject matter) are consistent with Cell.
journal_volume: Volume 184 is provided. Cell volume 184 (January–April 2022) is consistent with the DOI timestamp of late 2021/early 2022.
journal_issue: The journal name is given as 'Celp' — this is clearly a typographical error and should read 'Cell'. No issue number is explicitly provided. None (volume 184 of Cell existed in early 2022).
journal_page: No page numbers are provided. This is common in DOI-based citations for electronic journals where page numbers are replaced by article identifiers.
elocator: The volume number '184' is provided without issue number. Cell volume 184 corresponds to early 2022, which is consistent with the DOI year (2021 submission, 2022 publication). The elocator page number is missing but not always required in abbreviated citations.
publication_date: No explicit publication date is provided beyond the year implied by the DOI (2021) and PMCID. Cell papers with this volume would have been published in early 2022.
doi: The DOI (10.1016/j.cell.2021.11.031) follows the correct format for a Cell paper published by Elsevier. The prefix 'j.cell' is consistent with the journal Cell. The year component (2021) is plausible given the publication timeline. This appears to be a valid DOI.
pmcid: The PMCID (PMC8941949) follows the correct format with the 'PMC' prefix and numeric identifier. It is plausible for a paper published in Cell and indexed in PubMed Central.
pmid: The PMID (34910928) follows the correct format (numeric string). The number range is consistent with PubMed entries from late 2021/early 2022.
